preamble: The shift from the voiceless aspirated bilabial stop [pʰ] to zero [∅] is a form of elision. This trajectory is often found in the historical development of Indo-European languages where initial stops weakened into [h] before disappearing entirely.

examples.note: Historical trajectory: *p > pʰ > ɸ > h > ∅ (e.g. PIE *pəter > Old Irish 'athair').

sources: Ball, M. J., & Fife, J. (1993). The Celtic Languages.
